methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Charm Industrial is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Peter Reinhardt and Shaun Meehan that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (KoBold Metals at $3.0B Series C, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B Series C, and Amogy at $700M Series C), alongside 1 public comparable and 8 additional private peers in the Climate & Energy sector.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis across the 3-company comparable set produces a $1.0B median valuation, with a range of $700M to $3.0B reflecting stage and technology variance within the peer cohort.

The two methods yield a triangulated range of $162M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $595M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around which peer trajectory Charm Industrial most closely tracks—whether it converges toward earlier-stage metrics or commands later-stage multiples comparable to KoBold. Key variables include technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and near-term revenue trajectory. The median of $595M represents the central estimate across both methods.
